title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Under the direction of Hercule Poirot, Jeffrey Keene and Gregory Baring break down the locked door to Hubert Litcham Rock's study. Inside, they find Hubert slumped in a chair facing a window. A small pistol lies on the carpet near his limp hand, and Poirot observes a bullet hole in the right side of Hubert's head, with the exit wound shattering a mirror on the left wall. A piece of paper on the desk contains the word "sorry" written in shaky handwriting. Poirot discovers the room's key in the deceased man's pocket, and Harry Dahlhausen confirms the French window is latched. Poirot instructs the group to call the police but insists nothing be touched, noting that the gunshot was heard only fifteen minutes prior.

Poirot later speaks privately with Baring, a close friend of the deceased. Poirot reveals that Hubert had hired him in London to investigate a blackmail attempt involving a large sum of money. While Baring suggests the death was a suicide driven by eccentricity, Poirot remains skeptical. Baring mentions a new estate agent, Captain Marshall, a one-armed war veteran whom Hubert trusted. Baring dismisses rumors of Marshall swindling the estate, instead suggesting the focus should be on Diana Clifes, Lady Litcham Rock's adopted daughter. Baring admits he is in love with Diana and had Hubert's approval to marry her, though he describes her as a "little devil" who has had many relationships. Baring notes that Diana frequently sees Captain Marshall, leading Poirot to speculate that a connection between them might be the "family reason" Hubert wished to keep private.
